title: The Kingdom Divides
objective: Understand how Israel split into two kingdoms after Solomon.

sections.content: After Solomon's reign, the kingdom divided into Israel in the north and Judah in the south.

sections.text: So Israel has been in rebellion against the house of David to this day.

sections.heading: Division and Consequences
sections.content: The division weakened the nation and led to further spiritual decline.

questions: How did this affect Israel's faith?

sections.content: Lord, help Your people remain united in faith. Amen.

title: The Prophet Elijah
objective: Learn about Elijah's stand for God's truth.

sections.content: Elijah boldly proclaimed God's truth during a time of widespread idolatry.

sections.text: How long will you go limping between two different opinions?

sections.heading: Faithfulness in Difficult Times
sections.content: Elijah reminded Israel to return to the Lord alone.

questions: How can believers stand firm today?

sections.content: Lord, give us courage to stand for truth. Amen.

title: The Prophet Isaiah
objective: Understand Isaiah's message of warning and hope.

sections.content: Isaiah warned Judah about sin but also spoke powerful promises of a coming Savior.

sections.text: For to us a child is born, to us a son is given.

sections.heading: The Promise of the Messiah
sections.content: Isaiah foretold the coming of Jesus centuries before His birth.

questions: How do these prophecies point to Christ?

sections.content: Lord, thank You for the promises spoken through the prophets. Amen.

title: Jeremiah and the New Covenant
objective: Learn about Jeremiah's prophecy of a new covenant.

sections.content: Jeremiah spoke during a time when Judah was heading toward exile.

sections.text: I will put my law within them, and I will write it on their hearts.

sections.heading: A New Covenant Promise
sections.content: Jeremiah foretold a new covenant fulfilled through Jesus.

questions: How does Jesus fulfill this prophecy?

sections.content: Lord, write Your Word on our hearts. Amen.

title: The Fall of Jerusalem
objective: Understand the destruction of Jerusalem and the exile.

sections.content: Because of continued rebellion, Jerusalem was conquered and many people were taken into exile.

sections.text: He burned the house of the Lord.

sections.heading: Judgment for Sin
sections.content: The fall of Jerusalem showed the seriousness of rejecting God.

questions: How does God discipline His people?

sections.content: Lord, help us turn away from sin and follow You. Amen.
Daniel remained faithful to God while living in Babylon.

sections.text: He got down on his knees three times a day and prayed.

sections.heading: Faith in a Foreign Land
sections.content: Daniel's life shows how believers can remain faithful even in difficult environments.

questions: How can believers stay faithful today?

sections.content: Lord, strengthen us to remain faithful in every situation. Amen.

title: Return from Exile
objective: Understand how God restored His people after exile.

sections.content: Eventually God allowed the people of Judah to return and rebuild Jerusalem.

sections.text: Whoever is among you of all his people... let him go up to Jerusalem.

sections.heading: Restoration
sections.content: Even after judgment, God remained faithful to His covenant.

questions: Why were the people allowed to return?
What does this show about God's mercy?

sections.content: Lord, thank You for restoring Your people. Amen.

title: Waiting for the Messiah
objective: Recognize Israel's hope for the coming Savior.

sections.content: After returning from exile, God's people waited centuries for the promised Messiah.

sections.text: The sun of righteousness shall rise with healing in its wings.

sections.heading: Hope for Redemption
sections.content: The prophets reminded Israel that God would send the Savior.

questions: What promises kept their hope alive?
How were these fulfilled in Jesus?

sections.content: Lord Jesus, thank You for fulfilling the promises of salvation. Amen.
